title: The Brothers Karamazov

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "卡拉马佐夫兄弟" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: Alexei Fyodorovich Karamazov (Alyosha)
memes.description: The youngest Karamazov brother, a novice monk and the moral center of the novel, serving as confidant and mediator to virtually every major character.

memes.label: Captain Snegiryov
memes.description: The destitute, humiliated retired captain whose fierce love for his dying son Ilyusha makes him one of the novel's most tragically complex figures.

memes.label: Confession
memes.description: In The Brothers Karamazov, confession is the spiritual act through which hidden guilt becomes acknowledged truth — a process that the legal system's cross-examinations can only parody. The novel's crucial confessions (Dmitri's, Ivan's, Smerdyakov's) drive the moral drama that the trial's procedures cannot reach.

memes.label: Conscience
memes.description: "A central moral concept in The Brothers Karamazov — the inner voice that recognizes sin, the faculty of moral judgment, and the torment that drives characters toward either redemption or nihilism"

memes.label: Dmitri's Trial
memes.description: The public trial of Dmitri Karamazov for the murder of his father, a judicial spectacle in which circumstantial evidence, psychological manipulation, and the limitations of human justice converge to produce a catastrophic wrongful conviction. The novel's climactic public event, occupying the entirety of Book XII.

memes.label: Dmitri Fyodorovich Karamazov (Mitya)
memes.description: The eldest Karamazov brother, a passionate and impulsive former military officer torn between the "ideal of the Madonna" and the "ideal of Sodom," wrongly accused of his father's murder.

memes.label: Doubt
memes.description: "The intellectual and spiritual crisis of uncertainty regarding the existence of God, the immortality of the soul, and moral order — a central torment driving the novel's philosophical conflicts, embodied principally in Ivan Karamazov's rebellion and Dmitri's existential struggle."

memes.label: Elder (Starets)
memes.description: The Orthodox Christian institution of the elder (starets) — a spiritual guide endowed with the charism of discernment, exemplified by Father Zossima in The Brothers Karamazov. A living embodiment of the novel's theology of active love, universal responsibility, and the possibility of spiritual transformation through personal encounter.

memes.label: Evil
memes.description: "A central moral-theological concept in The Brothers Karamazov — the nature and origin of evil, the devil as a personification of human darkness, and the question of moral responsibility for evil acts"

memes.label: Faith
memes.description: "A central theological concept in The Brothers Karamazov — the nature of religious faith, its relationship to miracle and reason, and its role in moral life"

memes.label: Father Ferapont
memes.description: An ascetic monk at the monastery, rival and foil to Father Zossima — a fierce fasting hermit who claims to see devils and who embodies the dark side of monastic fanaticism.

memes.label: Father Zossima
memes.description: The venerable elder of the monastery, spiritual father to Alyosha Karamazov

memes.label: Father Zossima's Cell
memes.description: The private chamber of Elder Zossima within the hermitage at the monastery, where he receives visitors, offers spiritual counsel, and delivers his final teachings.

memes.label: Forgiveness
memes.description: A central moral practice in The Brothers Karamazov — the act of forgiving as a spiritual discipline that transcends legal categories of guilt and punishment, modeled by Christ's forgiveness and taught by Father Zossima as the foundation of Christian community.

memes.label: Freedom
memes.description: "A central concept in The Brothers Karamazov explored through the characters' moral and spiritual struggles"

memes.label: Fyodor Pavlovitch Karamazov
memes.description: The dissolute patriarch of the Karamazov family, whose murder drives the plot of the novel

memes.label: Fyodor Pavlovitch's House
memes.description: The residence of Fyodor Pavlovitch Karamazov in Skotoprigonyevsk — a house of debauchery, greed, and domestic violence that serves as the dark center of the Karamazov family tragedy.

memes.label: Grace
memes.description: "The unmerited divine gift of forgiveness, love, and spiritual renewal that flows through small acts of compassion — a single onion, a word from the heart, a kiss — in Dostoevsky's theological vision."

memes.label: Agrafena Alexandrovna Svetlov
memes.description: The beautiful and calculating young woman at the center of the novel's love triangle — desired by both Fyodor Pavlovich and his son Dmitri, yet capable of unexpected spiritual depth and redemption.

memes.label: Grushenka's House
memes.description: The residence of Agrafena Alexandrovna Svetlov (Grushenka) in Skotoprigonyevsk, owned by the merchant Samsonov. A place of complex seduction, rivalry, and unexpected generosity.

memes.label: Guilt
memes.description: "A central concept in The Brothers Karamazov explored through the characters' moral and spiritual struggles"

memes.label: Humility
memes.description: "A core spiritual virtue in The Brothers Karamazov — the paradoxical power of self-emptying love, contrasted with pride and explored through the teachings of Father Zossima and the transformations of key characters"

memes.label: Ilyusha Snegiryov
memes.description: The young, dying son of Captain Snegiryov, whose suffering and death unite the schoolboys and inspire Alyosha's famous speech at the stone.

memes.label: Ilyusha's Funeral
memes.description: The funeral of the schoolboy Ilyusha Snegiryov and Alyosha's speech at the stone, in which he urges the boys to remember their shared love and the goodness of this day, transforming a child's death into a sacrament of communal memory.

memes.label: Immortality
memes.description: "A central concept in The Brothers Karamazov explored through the characters' moral and spiritual struggles"

memes.label: Ivan Fyodorovich Karamazov
memes.description: The middle Karamazov brother, a brilliant intellectual and atheist whose philosophical rebellion against God and the moral order sets the novel's central tragedy in motion.

memes.label: Justice
memes.description: A central tension in The Brothers Karamazov — the conflict between human legal justice, which relies on evidence and procedure but can convict the innocent, and divine justice, which operates through conscience, confession, and suffering as a path to redemption.

memes.label: Katerina Ivanovna Verkhovtsev
memes.description: Dmitri Karamazov's betrothed, a proud and aristocratic young woman whose obsessive love, wounded pride, and moral theatricality entangle her with both Ivan and Dmitri in a destructive triangle.

memes.label: Katerina Ivanovna's House
memes.description: The residence of Katerina Ivanovna Verkhovtsev in Skotoprigonyevsk, where she stays with Madame Hohlakov and Lise. A setting for scenes of pride, moral anguish, and tangled romantic entanglements.

memes.label: Kolya Krasotkin
memes.description: The precocious, proud leader of the schoolboys, whose intellectual arrogance is humbled through his friendship with the dying Ilyusha and his respect for Alyosha.

memes.label: Lise Khokhlakov
memes.description: The young, impish daughter of Madame Khokhlakov, betrothed to Alyosha, whose playful exterior conceals a troubling inner turmoil.

memes.label: Love
memes.description: "A central theological and moral concept in The Brothers Karamazov — love as an active, laborious force that redeems, transforms, and binds humanity to God and to one another"

memes.label: Love
memes.description: "A central theological and moral concept in The Brothers Karamazov — love as an active, laborious force that redeems, transforms, and binds humanity to God and to one another"

memes.label: Madame Khokhlakov
memes.description: The wealthy, garrulous, and absurdly inconsistent widow whose comedic meddling in the lives of the major characters provides some of the novel's sharpest satire of high society.

memes.label: Mikhail Rakitin
memes.description: A divinity student and would-be journalist, Rakitin is the novel's cynic — a petty, envious schemer who cloaks his opportunism in progressive rhetoric and serves as a foil to Alyosha's spiritual integrity.

memes.label: Miracle
memes.description: "A central concept in The Brothers Karamazov explored through the characters' moral and spiritual struggles"

memes.label: Mokroe
memes.description: A village and inn located some distance from Skotoprigonyevsk, where Dmitri Karamazov stages two orgiastic sprees and where he is arrested for the murder of his father.

memes.label: Obedience
memes.description: "The practice of voluntary renunciation of one's own will to an elder, understood by Dostoevsky as a path from slavery to freedom and a central discipline of the monastic life"

memes.label: Pride
memes.description: "A central moral-psychological concept in The Brothers Karamazov — pride as the root of spiritual bondage, the antithesis of humility, and the sin that masquerades as virtue across the Karamazov family"

memes.label: Sin
memes.description: "A central theological concept in The Brothers Karamazov — the nature of sin as universal human condition, the relationship between sin and responsibility, and the possibility of redemption through active love"

memes.label: Pavel Smerdyakov
memes.description: Fyodor Pavlovich's illegitimate son, servant in his own father's house, and the novel's central antagonist — a morose, epileptic young man whose intellectual pride and resentment culminate in parricide.

memes.label: Suffering
memes.description: "A central concept in The Brothers Karamazov explored through the characters' moral and spiritual struggles"

memes.label: The Brass Pestle
memes.description: A small brass pestle from a mortar in the kitchen of Fyodor Pavlovitch's house, seized by Dmitri as a weapon, used to strike the servant Grigory, and later presented as physical evidence of Dmitri's violent presence at the murder scene. An ordinary domestic object transformed into a symbol of impulsive violence and circumstantial proof.

memes.label: The Court
memes.description: The judicial institution in The Brothers Karamazov that tries Dmitri for the murder of his father — a vehicle for Dostoevsky's critique of legal rationalism and its inability to grasp moral and spiritual truth.

memes.label: The Courthouse
memes.description: The courthouse in Skotoprigonyevsk where Dmitri Karamazov stands trial for the murder of his father. The setting for the novel's climactic legal drama and Dostoevsky's exploration of justice, truth, and the limits of rational inquiry.

memes.label: The Envelope
memes.description: The envelope in which Fyodor Pavlovitch prepared three thousand roubles as a lure for Grushenka — sealed with five seals and tied with ribbon, addressed to her, and later found empty on the night of the murder. A central piece of physical evidence whose absence of contents becomes as significant as its existence.

memes.label: The Gathering at the Monastery
memes.description: The fateful gathering of the Karamazov family and their associates in Father Zossima's cell at the monastery, where long-simmering conflicts erupt into public scandal.

memes.label: The Grand Inquisitor
memes.description: Ivan Karamazov's prose poem, told to Alyosha, in which Christ returns to 16th-century Seville during the Inquisition and is confronted by the Grand Inquisitor, who condemns Him for granting humanity the unbearable burden of freedom.
aliases: the legend of the Grand Inquisitor

memes.label: The Hermitage
memes.description: The secluded section of the monastery grounds where Elder Zossima resides, separated from the main monastery by a pine wood. The setting for Zossima's final teachings and Alyosha's transformative vision of the wedding at Cana.

memes.label: The Jury
memes.description: The twelve-man jury in Dmitri Karamazov's trial — representing "the people" as arbiters of justice, whose verdict becomes the novel's most consequential moral failure of institutional authority.

memes.label: The Market Square
memes.description: The central public space of Skotoprigonyevsk, where crowds gather, news spreads, and significant encounters occur. The site of key trial-related scenes and public life in the provincial town.

memes.label: The Monastery
memes.description: The monastery located near Skotoprigonyevsk, home to the renowned Elder Zossima and Alyosha Karamazov during his novitiate. A spiritual center where pivotal confrontations, revelations, and the novel's central religious debates unfold.

memes.label: The Murder of Fyodor Karamazov
memes.description: The murder of the patriarch Fyodor Pavlovitch Karamazov by his illegitimate son Smerdyakov, for which Dmitri is wrongly arrested, tried, and convicted in one of literature's most famous miscarriages of justice. The crime that transforms the novel's philosophical debates about guilt and responsibility into criminal reality.

memes.label: The Prison
memes.description: The prison in Skotoprigonyevsk where Dmitri Karamazov is held during his trial for the murder of his father. A place of confinement that paradoxically becomes the setting for his spiritual awakening.

memes.label: The Prosecutor's Speech
memes.description: The prosecutor Ippolit Kirillovitch's lengthy courtroom speech at Dmitri's trial, a masterwork of psychological portraiture that constructs a devastating case against Dmitri by interpreting the "Karamazov nature" as inherently criminal, while also serving as Dostoevsky's critique of rationalist reduction.

memes.label: The Rebellion
memes.description: Ivan Karamazov's philosophical rebellion against God, in which he returns his "ticket" to heaven by cataloguing the horrific suffering of innocent children, arguing that no future harmony can justify their pain.

memes.label: The Soul
memes.description: A foundational concept in The Brothers Karamazov — the immortal soul as the locus of moral struggle between good and evil, the object of both divine love and demonic temptation, and the irreducible human reality that legal and scientific rationalism cannot account for.

memes.label: The Three Thousand Roubles
memes.description: The sum of money that drives the novel's central conflict — owed by Dmitri to Katerina Ivanovna, sealed in an envelope by Fyodor Pavlovitch as a lure for Grushenka, and cited as Dmitri's motive for murder. A physical token of the entanglement of money, desire, and guilt in The Brothers Karamazov.

memes.label: Truth
memes.description: "A central thematic tension in The Brothers Karamazov between divine, revealed truth (istina) as embodied by Zossima and lived by Alyosha, and human, rational truth-seeking that leads Ivan into despair — expressed through the novel's exploration of whether truth can be known through faith, reason, or suffering."

memes.label: Zossima's Death
memes.description: The death of the revered Elder Zossima, his final teachings to his disciples, and the subsequent scandal of his body's premature corruption, which shakes the faith of the monastery and deeply tests Alyosha.
